food_subgroup: Beef — grilled steak with salad side

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002725 covers beef steak food product as closest parent; no specific ribeye steak with salad entry exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Beef meat, grilled) is the closest parent; no specific ribeye-with-salad composite code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226847009 covers beef steak generically; no ribeye-specific or composite meal code available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical restaurant serving consists of approximately 250 g grilled ribeye steak and 100 g mixed salad, totalling around 350 g per plate.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, ribeye steak, boneless, lip-off, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, cooked, grilled (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #23161) combined with mixed salad greens (USDA FoodData Central #11959) in a 250 g steak / 100 g salad ratio
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry exists for ribeye steak with salad. Values are derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled ribeye steak (FNDDS #23161) and mixed salad greens (#11959), weighted at 71.4% steak and 28.6% salad to reflect a 250 g + 100 g restaurant portion, then recalculated to per-100 g basis. Sodium adjusted upward to reflect typical restaurant seasoning (salt, pepper, light sauce) based on USDA Branded Food analogues.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Ribeye steak is a protein-and-fat dominant food with negligible digestible carbohydrate (~1.8 g per 100 g, ~6.3 g per 350 g serving). Per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021, pure beef and non-starchy salad greens have a GI that is effectively zero because they contain insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response. Following Veritas v2 critical rule for zero-GI foods, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (1 × 6.3) / 100 = 0.063.

hypertension.guidance: A 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 1085 mg sodium, representing 47% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should request reduced seasoning or sauce on the side.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible digestible carbohydrate and very low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only approximately 6.3 g of carbohydrate per serving and a glycemic load of 0.063, grilled ribeye steak with salad has minimal impact on postprandial blood glucose and is generally suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided high-sugar dressings are avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A 350 g serving delivers approximately 15.8 g of saturated fat (79% of the 20 g daily limit for a 2000 kcal diet) and approximately 252 mg cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ribeye Steak with Salad is a Western-style grilled beef dish served at restaurants in Singapore, including Vietnamese-fusion establishments. The ribeye cut is taken from the rib section of the beef, prized for its generous marbling of intramuscular fat which gives it a rich, buttery flavour and tender, juicy texture when grilled to medium or medium-rare. The steak is typically seasoned with salt, black pepper, and sometimes a light herb butter or sauce, then cooked on a high-heat grill or cast-iron surface to develop a caramelised crust. It is served alongside a mixed green salad that may include lettuce, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, and a light vinaigrette or creamy dressing. Nutritionally, the dish is high in complete protein (approximately 21.8 g per 100 g), moderate in fat (11.4 g per 100 g, of which 4.5 g is saturated), and very low in carbohydrate (1.8 g per 100 g), making it a low-glycemic meal. It is a significant source of zinc, iron, vitamin B12, and selenium. The high protein and very low carbohydrate profile (glycemic load of 0.063 per serving) make it broadly suitable for low-carbohydrate dietary patterns and for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate to high depending on restaurant seasoning, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans.
common_user_queries: Calories in ribeye steak with salad
Is ribeye steak good for diabetics?
How much protein in ribeye steak?
Can I eat steak if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for ribeye steak with salad as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled ribeye steak and mixed salad greens, weighted by a representative restaurant portion ratio. The adaptation is methodologically sound but introduces estimation uncertainty, warranting medium rather than high confidence.
notes: 1. food_group corrected from input value 'Vegetables' to 'Meat and alternatives' as the primary component is grilled beef. 2. GI is recorded as 1 (the lowest valid positive value) because ribeye steak and non-starchy salad greens contain insufficient digestible carbohydrate to produce a measurable glycemic response; this is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database conventions for protein-and-fat dominant foods and Veritas v2 critical rules for zero-GI foods. gi_category is set to 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.063. 3. Sodium value of 310 mg per 100 g reflects typical restaurant seasoning; unseasoned grilled ribeye would be approximately 65 mg per 100 g per USDA SR Legacy. 4. trans_fat_g of 0.3 g per 100 g reflects naturally occurring conjugated linoleic acid (CLA) and vaccenic acid present in ruminant beef fat, not industrially produced trans fat; this is per USDA SR Legacy data. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B-vitamins are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy weighted composite and are not -1 as they are reportable from the approved source. 6. The restaurant serving this dish (Viet'Spice, Circular Road) is a Vietnamese-fusion establishment; the dish itself is a Western-style grilled steak and is classified under Singapore cuisine as served in Singapore. 7. Halal status is not confirmed as ribeye steak sourcing and preparation method at the specific restaurant are unknown.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' to 'Meat and alternatives'.
Derived per_100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy weighted composite (250 g ribeye + 100 g salad).
Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Populated aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check performed.

Updated glycemic_index from 0 to 1 and recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to 0.063, adhering to Veritas v2 critical rule for zero-GI foods with non-zero carbohydrates. Justification updated in gi_source and notes.
Added three health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Added honest ontology codes with closest_parent match_type and gap notes.
Documented all sentinel value usage and sodium adjustment rationale in notes.
